blocks.text: Surgery/ Date: DOS: 03/12/2026 Right Total Knee Arthroplasty
Homebound: medically restricted, taxing effort to leave the home due to surgical intervention as
documented in hospital and clinical visit notes. Pt needs the use of assistance device and/or help from
another person in order to leave home.
I certify this patient is confined to home and needs intermittent skilled nursing care and or therapy
The patient is under my care & I have authorized the services on this plan
Home Care and Home Infusion Orders
PT - pre-operative patient/home evaluation (up to 14 days in advance of the procedure)
Provide anaphylactic kit per pharmacist recommendations as needed.
RN - standing orders for home infusion medication administration protocol x2 on POD 0.
RN - assessment and skilled intervention/care related to infusion therapy, vascular access site and
multisystem assessment. Monitor patient's response to therapy and for side effects/complications to
include, medication, pain and incision monitoring.
RN - to start/maintain IV catheter per protocol and remove at the conclusion of therapy.
RN - has patient start oral pain meds as soon as patient tolerates PO intake.
RN - may straight cath PRN inability to void post op
RN - Remove dressing POD 3 and 7, may clean with hydrogen peroxide prn. Apply dry dressing, may
repeat as needed for saturation. Leave dry dressing in place after POD 7 until MD f/u appt.
RN - per protocol to assess for pain, incision monitoring and post op complications.
RN - to instruct patient to take ASA 325mg PO BID x4-6 wks starting POD 1, unless otherwise stated

Since last exam, this patient's condition has: Worsened.
A review of systems was performed and was all negative except for the system as documented on
the initial visit, those systems associated with the injury, and any changes noted below.
R knee OA, pain continues, no significant relief W conservative, management W NSAIDS<
injections steroid and HA series, had arthroscopic surgery 2 yrs ago , MM tear and OA noted, has
Julianne returns for a Recheck and the condition has; Worsened.
Past Medical History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Past Surgical History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Family History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Social History: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Allergies: NKDA. Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Review of Systems: Unchanged Since Last Visit.
Constitutional: The patient appears to be stated age, cooperative and in no distress. The patient
is able to answer questions and carry on a conversation without use of accessory muscles of
respiration. The patient is well nourished and hydrated with moist mucous membranes.
Eye: Externally: Normal appearance of the conjunctiva, eyelids with normal ocular symmetry.
Sclera are normal without hemorrhage or icterus. Pupils are equal round and reactive to light and
Ears, Nose, Mouth, Throat: Normal external appearance of the ears and nose. Normal bilateral
auditory acuity. Normal appearance of the oropharynx without pallor or cyanosis and with moist
mucosa. Normal appearance of the teeth and gums without and dental caries. Normal appearance
of the palate with normal appearance rise of the uvula.
Neck: No jugular venous elevation. Normal thyroid exam without enlargement, tenderness or
nodules. Normal parotid exam without enlargement, tenderness or nodules. No palpable masses
or nodules. No palpable No palpable lymphadenopathy in the anterior cervical, posterior cervical,
Respiratory: Normal respiratory effort without use of accessory muscles of respiration or pursed
lip breathing. Normal external appearance of the chest without barrel chest, pectus, kyphosis or
scoliosis. Normal breath sounds without wheezing, rhonchi or rales. Normal percussive exam
without dullness or evidence of consolidation.
Lymphatics: Lymph Nodes Normal. No evidence of cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal
Musculoskeletal: R knee MJL pain, spurring painful, no laxity, good AROM, no effusion, antalgic
Extremities: Normal appearance of the upper and lower extremities without obvious deformity.
There is no digital clubbing, cyanosis or edema.
Skin: Externally there is no evidence of xanthomas or cellulitis. This is no dermatitis or rash. There are no
ulcers or wounds present. There is no evidence of venous stasis changes. The capillary refill is < 5 seconds.
Neurological/Psychiatric: Externally the face is symmetrical. There is normal sharp and dull
sensation in the hands and feet bilaterally. The cranial nerves are intact II - XII. The patient moves
the upper and lower extremities equally. The finger to nose exam is normal. Appropriate mood and
affect. Alert and oriented to person, time and place. Patient appears to have intact judgement and
insight into the current health issues.
MRI R knee W tricompartmental OA,
